---
title: "The Bridgerton Baby Names"
author: "Deepali Kank"
date: 2026-06-16
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

```{r}
#| label: setup

# Packages ---------------------------------------------------------------

library(tidyverse)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)

# Data -------------------------------------------------------------------

data_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2026/2026-06-16"

all_names <- readr::read_csv(
  stringr::str_c(data_url, "/england_wales_names.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
  janitor::clean_names() |>
  mutate(name = str_to_title(name))

bridgerton_names <- tribble(
  ~name,        ~flower_tone, ~name_order,
  "Violet",    "violet",      1,
  "Eloise",    "leaf",        2,
  "Francesca", "rose",        3,
  "Daphne",    "petal",       4,
  "Hyacinth",  "ivory",       5
)

bridgerton_data <- all_names |>
  filter(sex == "Girl", year >= 1996) |>
  inner_join(bridgerton_names, by = join_by(name), unmatched = "drop")

bridgerton_summary <- bridgerton_data |>
  summarise(
    rank_2024 = rank[year == 2024][1],
    peak_number = max(number, na.rm = TRUE),
    peak_year = year[which.max(number)],
    .by = c(name, flower_tone, name_order)
  ) |>
  arrange(name_order) |>
  mutate(
    rank_2024 = replace_na(rank_2024, NA_real_),
    name = fct_reorder(name, name_order, .desc = TRUE)
  )

plot_data <- bridgerton_data |>
  select(year, name, number) |>
  complete(
    year = full_seq(year, 1),
    name = bridgerton_names$name,
    fill = list(number = 0)
  ) |>
  left_join(bridgerton_names, by = join_by(name), relationship = "many-to-one") |>
  left_join(
    bridgerton_summary |>
      mutate(name = as.character(name)) |>
      select(name, rank_2024, peak_number, peak_year),
    by = join_by(name),
    relationship = "many-to-one"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    name = fct_reorder(name, name_order, .desc = TRUE),
    size_number = if_else(number > 0, number, NA_real_),
    peak_label = str_glue("{scales::comma(peak_number)} in {peak_year}")
  )

bridgerton_peaks <- plot_data |>
  filter(year == peak_year, number == peak_number)

bridgerton_2024 <- plot_data |>
  filter(year == 2024)

# Miscs ------------------------------------------------------------------

font_display <- "Snell Roundhand"
font_body <- "Avenir"

color_paper <- "#f4edf6"
color_ink <- "#2f2731"
color_violet_dark <- "#4a2d62"
color_muted <- "#857585"
color_stem <- "#b7b9a3"
color_bridgerton <- c(
  ivory = "#efe8b9",
  leaf = "#9fb0a6",
  petal = "#8fb0c6",
  rose = "#d9a7c7",
  violet = "#7c8faa"
)
color_bridgerton_text <- c(
  ivory = "#b79a3f",
  leaf = "#6d8176",
  petal = "#4f8ea5",
  rose = "#b86598",
  violet = "#647994"
)

# Texts ------------------------------------------------------------------

title_text <- "<span style='color:#4a2d62'>The Bridgerton</span> baby names: old peaks, new blooms"
subtitle_text <- str_glue(
  "Annual girls' births in England & Wales from 1996-2024 show very different arcs: <b>Violet</b> rose into the top 50, ",
  "<b>Francesca</b> peaked early in 1998.<br><b>Daphne</b> reached its best year in 2024, ",
  "and <b>Hyacinth</b> appears only as a tiny 2024 debut with 3 births."
)
caption_text <- str_glue(
  "Source: Office for National Statistics via TidyTuesday 2026 W24. ",
  "Annual births for girls in England and Wales, 1996-2024."
)

# Plots ------------------------------------------------------------------

theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 11, base_family = font_body))
```

```{=html}
<style>
body,
#quarto-content,
main,
.page-columns,
.content,
.column-page,
.column-body {
  background: #f4edf6;
  color: #2f2731;
}

.quarto-title-block {
  display: none;
}
</style>
```

```{r}
#| label: bridgerton-history
#| fig-width: 13
#| fig-height: 7.8

p <- plot_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = name)) +
  annotate(
    "rect",
    xmin = 2020,
    xmax = 2024.8,
    ymin = -Inf,
    ymax = Inf,
    fill = "#eadfed",
    alpha = 0.45
  ) +
  geom_line(
    aes(group = name),
    color = color_stem,
    linewidth = 0.55,
    alpha = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = \(x) filter(x, !is.na(size_number)),
    aes(size = size_number, fill = flower_tone),
    shape = 21,
    color = color_paper,
    stroke = 0.35,
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = bridgerton_peaks,
    aes(fill = flower_tone),
    shape = 21,
    color = color_ink,
    stroke = 0.5,
    size = 4.4
  ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = bridgerton_peaks,
    aes(label = peak_label, color = flower_tone),
    family = font_body,
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3,
    direction = "x",
    nudge_y = 0.35,
    segment.color = color_stem,
    segment.size = 0.2,
    box.padding = 0.25,
    min.segment.length = 0,
    seed = 24
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = bridgerton_2024,
    aes(fill = flower_tone),
    shape = 21,
    color = color_ink,
    stroke = 0.5,
    size = 4.4
  ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = bridgerton_2024,
    aes(
      label = str_glue("#{scales::comma(rank_2024)} · {scales::comma(number)} births"),
      color = flower_tone
    ),
    family = font_body,
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.2,
    direction = "y",
    hjust = 0,
    nudge_x = 1.25,
    segment.color = color_stem,
    segment.size = 0.2,
    box.padding = 0.25,
    min.segment.length = 0,
    seed = 16
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2022.45,
    y = 5.62,
    label = "recent seasons",
    family = font_display,
    color = color_muted,
    fontface = "italic",
    size = 3.6
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(1996, 2004, 2012, 2020, 2024),
    limits = c(1995.4, 2029),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(0.65, 0.85))) +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 15, breaks = c(3, 250, 500, 1000), guide = "none") +
  scale_color_manual(values = color_bridgerton_text) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = color_bridgerton) +
  labs(
    title = title_text,
    subtitle = subtitle_text,
    caption = caption_text,
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = color_paper, color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = color_paper, color = NA),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(color = color_ink, size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = color_ink, face = "bold", size = 13),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown(
      color = color_violet_dark,
      face = "bold",
      family = font_display,
      size = 38,
      margin = margin(b = 7)
    ),
    plot.subtitle = ggtext::element_markdown(
      color = color_ink,
      size = 12,
      lineheight = 1.2,
      margin = margin(b = 18)
    ),
    plot.caption = element_text(color = color_muted, size = 8, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.margin = margin(18, 22, 14, 22)
  )

p
```
